chunks.text: IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH BENCH AT SUKKUR Constitutional Petition No.S-103 of 2026 [Mst. Sumera and another vs. the Province of Sindh and others.] Date Order with the signature of the Judge 1. For orders on CMA No.313/2026 (U/A). 2. For orders on office objections. 3. For orders on CMA No.414/2026 (Ex/A). 4. For the hearing of the main case. 27.04.2026. Mr. Abdul Samad Shar, Advocate for Petitioners. ******************* The petitioners have filed the instant petition seeking directions to the respondents not to cause any sort of harassment, not to lodge any FIR against them, and to provide protection. It is an admitted position that before filing the present petition, the petitioners had approached the learned Additional Sessions Judge/Ex-Officio Justice of the Peace, Kandiaro, by filing Criminal Miscellaneous Application No. 4021 of 2025, which was disposed of vide order dated 06.08.2025, whereby protection was granted, and the concerned police officials were directed not to harass the petitioners. The grievance of the petitioners is that, despite the aforesaid directions, the police functionaries have neither provided protection nor refrained from alleged harassment. At the outset, it may be observed that in exercise of Constitutional jurisdiction under Article 199 of the Constitution of the Islamic Republic of Pakistan, this Court does not act as an executing forum for implementation of orders passed by the Justice of the Peace. The remedy available to the petitioners lies before the same forum, as under Section 22-A(iii), Cr.P.C., the Justice of the Peace is competent to pass appropriate orders in cases of excess or abuse by police officials.

At the outset, it may be observed that in exercise of Constitutional jurisdiction under Article 199 of the Constitution of the Islamic Republic of Pakistan, this Court does not act as an executing forum for implementation of orders passed by the Justice of the Peace. The remedy available to the petitioners lies before the same forum, as under Section 22-A(iii), Cr.P.C., the Justice of the Peace is competent to pass appropriate orders in cases of excess or abuse by police officials. 2 Learned counsel for the petitioners has failed to offer any plausible explanation as to why the petitioners did not avail the said remedy for redressal of their grievance. In these circumstances, since an adequate and efficacious remedy is available to the petitioners, the present petition is not maintainable. Accordingly, the same is dismissed. J U D G E
